title: Silence MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warnings on ESM planning scripts
description: Node.js emits MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON on every run of scripts/planning/*.js because these ESM files sit under a package.json that lacks "type":"module". Fix: add marker files scripts/planning/package.json + scripts/planning/lib/package.json, each {"type":"module"}. SoNash carries the lib one already. Non-blocking; cosmetic noise only.

title: SoNash file registry — full migration-opportunity map
description: Build a systematic registry mapping every file in SoNash with: source path, upstream callers, downstream deps, workflows touched, sanitization hits (per PORT_ANALYSIS extended regex), migration necessity verdict (port / sanitize-port / redesign / skip / defer), and rationale. Input may come from an outside source (external repo, CLI tool, or standalone generator). Goal: replace ad-hoc per-port MI-1 pre-analysis with a one-shot comprehensive scan that feeds sync-mechanism research (MI-3) AND guides Layer 2/3/4 gating decisions. Natural pair with sync-mechanism brainstorm and the propagation-patterns subsystem (D37).

title: CLAUDE.md §3 Architecture fill-in
description: Populate CLAUDE.md §3 Architecture section (currently TBD placeholder). Deferred per D4 during Foundation discovery — §1 Stack and §2 Security landed in Layer 0+ item 0j, but §3 was held until architecture emerges from sync-mechanism research. Trigger: post-sync-mechanism-research, when the propagation/sync/memory architecture has actually taken shape in the repo.

title: Memory bidirectional sync
description: Implement bidirectional sync for .claude/canonical-memory/ ↔ ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/memory/. Currently D27 landed one-way canonical-first with manual cp as onboarding step. Research caveat CH-C-010 notes session-local memory fix does not solve portability. Trigger: sync-mechanism research addresses memory as part of core scope — natural pairing since both are propagate-changes-across-locations problems.

title: Port _shared/SELF_AUDIT_PATTERN.md + _shared/SKILL_STANDARDS.md from SoNash
description: 7 references from skill-audit SKILL.md and REFERENCE.md point into .claude/skills/_shared/ which does not exist in JASON-OS (dead links). Raised 2026-04-17 during Layer 0+ item 0f port. Options: (A) port both files from SoNash, (B) rewrite refs to drop _shared/ paths (content duplication). Recommendation A. Trigger: Layer 3 engagement (SKILL_INDEX.md pairs naturally with _shared/) OR first skill port needing SELF_AUDIT_PATTERN or SKILL_STANDARDS for MI-5 sub-step.

title: Port or rewrite npm run skills:validate refs in skill-audit
description: 5 advisory references in skill-audit/SKILL.md point to a SoNash npm script not in JASON-OS. Currently non-blocking (advisory if-you-have-this-command prompts). Users running skill-audit will see missing-npm-script errors if they follow the refs. Options: (A) port skills:validate to JASON-OS as Layer 3 validation tooling, (B) rewrite refs with manual equivalents, (C) skill-audit refresh from later SoNash branch with stack-agnostic equivalent. Raised 2026-04-17 during 0f.

title: Gemini CLI integration in /pr-review
description: Add Gemini as a third AI reviewer voice (alongside Qodo + SonarCloud) in /pr-review. Deferred per D23: minimum set for MI-4 was Qodo+SonarCloud only. CodeRabbit excluded (durable preference — SoNash experience with overlap/noise). Gemini CLI v0.38.1 already installed locally; OAuth pending (user-action). Trigger: after Qodo+SonarCloud stabilized and user wants another reviewer voice.

title: pr-retro skill port
description: Port pr-retro from SoNash (deferred in .planning/jason-os/BOOTSTRAP_DEFERRED.md during original Foundation bootstrap). Trigger: pr-ecosystem-audit skill exists (coupling needed for full SoNash version) OR pr-retro simplified to a standalone retro skill that does not require pr-ecosystem-audit.

title: synthesize + recall skill ports from SoNash
description: Neither skill currently exists in JASON-OS .claude/skills/ (which holds 9 skills + add-debt stub). SoNash has active uncommitted upgrade work on both (second-locale work) as of Step 2 check. Not in Foundation firm or gated scope. Raised 2026-04-17 during Step 2. Trigger: (a) SoNash upstream commits land for synthesize + recall — user will signal; AND (b) JASON-OS scope expands to include either — probably coupled to sync-mechanism research per MI-3 since synthesize specifically touches the propagation pipeline.

title: runAnalyze mandatory mode flip
description: user-prompt-handler currently advisory per D6 + D25 (stderr hints only). Flip to mandatory mode via env var JASON_ANALYZE_MODE=mandatory (1-line change thanks to D25 parameterized design — dispatch table extracted to .claude/analyze-directives.json). Trigger: ≥5 agents have PROACTIVELY clauses stable + workflow pattern stabilized (≥10 sessions using advisory mode).

title: Husky pre-commit enrichment (14 deferred checks)
description: Composite entry for 14 deferred pre-commit checks from D36-revised: ESLint, tests, lint-staged, pattern-compliance, propagation-staged, audit-s0s1, skill-validation, cognitive-complexity, cross-doc-deps, doc-headers, doc-index auto-update, agent compliance (non-blocking), debt-schema, JSONL-MD sync. Layer 0+ item 0h laid down the husky scaffold so each addition is single-line. Per-item triggers (e.g., ESLint when stack declared JS/TS; audit-s0s1 when audit system lands). Break into individual todos as each trigger fires.

title: Husky pre-push enrichment (11 deferred checks)
description: Composite entry for 11 deferred pre-push checks from D36-revised: circular-deps, pattern-compliance-push, code-reviewer gate, propagation full, hook-tests, security-check, tsc, cyclomatic-CC, cognitive-CC, npm-audit, event-triggers. Husky scaffold (0h) + escalation-gate stanza already present. Per-item triggers (e.g., tsc when TypeScript stack; code-reviewer gate when track-agent-invocation ported in Layer 2). Break into individual todos as each trigger fires.

title: Pattern cognition & propagation subsystem — full port (D37)
description: Full port of SoNash subsystem: 8 scripts + JSON registry + test suite + 7 npm scripts. Seeded by Layer 0+ item 0i (scripts/config/propagation-patterns.seed.json with 4 anti-patterns from CLAUDE.md §5). Pairs naturally with sync-mechanism research per MI-3 — both are propagate-changes-across-locations. Trigger: After Foundation firm layers complete AND sync-mechanism research begins.

title: session-end-commit.js SESSION_CONTEXT.md format compatibility
description:
Ported session-end-commit.js from SoNash 41526 expects the literal string `**Uncommitted Work**: Yes` (inline bold-colon format) but JASON-OS SESSION_CONTEXT.md uses the SoNash 5-field `## Header
Value` format per D12. Script logs `Could not find Uncommitted Work field` and returns false. Fix: either (a) update the script to parse the `## Uncommitted Work
<value>` format, or (b) rewrite a JASON-OS-native session-end-commit.js. Discovered during first real /session-end invocation 2026-04-17. Non-blocking — main session currently handles session-end git orchestration directly.

title: Validate commit-tracker + pre-compaction-save hooks fire under live Claude Code invocation
description: Session 2 fixed a stdin/argv mismatch in both Layer 1 hooks (commit 4db5afb): they were ported from SoNash reading process.argv[2], but Claude Code passes hook context as JSON via stdin. All 4 manual stdin scenarios pass and artifacts (commit-log.jsonl, handoff.json) write correctly. Could not validate live in Session 2 because Claude Code caches the hook config from settings.json at session start, and this session started on the pre-Layer-1 startup-41526 branch. After next Claude Code restart on bootstrap-41726, validate by: (1) make any commit, verify .claude/state/commit-log.jsonl appends a new entry; (2) for PreCompact, harder to trigger naturally — verify .claude/state/handoff.json appears after natural compaction or via /checkpoint. If either fails, the next debugging vector is the if: clause in settings.json (PostToolUse Bash) — may not be valid Claude Code syntax; falling back to no if: + script-internal regex filter is the simplest workaround.

title: settings-guardian.js fires on all Write/Edit but blocks non-settings.json writes
description: PreToolUse Write/Edit hook (matcher ^([Ww]rite|[Ee]dit)$) invokes settings-guardian.js for every Write/Edit tool call. The script then compares the new content against the live .claude/settings.json hook list and blocks if critical hooks (block-push-to-main.js, settings-guardian.js) appear absent. For Write calls targeting NON-settings.json files (e.g., a fresh state file in .claude/state/), the new content has no hooks at all, so the script perceives all critical hooks as removed and blocks the write. Discovered 2026-04-17 during PR #3 R2 wrap-up — Write tool blocked when creating .claude/state/task-pr-review-3-r2.state.json; worked around via Bash heredoc. Fix: add a path check at the top of settings-guardian — if the target file path is not the project settings.json (or settings.local.json), exit 0 immediately. Pre-existing in SoNash port (file copied as-is from 41526). Also a SoNash backport candidate.

title: session-end-commit.js scope mismatch with skill Step 3
description: The /session-end skill Step 3 instructs Claude to update PLAN.md/PORT_ANALYSIS.md when a plan advanced, but scripts/session-end-commit.js at Step 10 only stages and commits SESSION_CONTEXT.md. Plan file updates get left as stragglers needing a follow-up commit. Session 3 hit this: 4def022 committed SESSION_CONTEXT.md, 6e88fa1 had to commit PLAN.md separately. Options: (a) teach the script to stage and commit plan file changes too, (b) pre-stage plan files before Step 10 so session-end-commit.js picks them up in the single commit, (c) formalize multi-commit session-end in the skill.
progress: Fixed Session 10 (2026-04-20) via option (a): added SESSION_END_PATHSPECS allowlist (SESSION_CONTEXT.md + .planning/**/PLAN.md + .planning/**/PORT_ANALYSIS.md) using git :(glob) magic pathspec; replaced hasSessionContextChanges() with getModifiedSessionEndFiles() parsing porcelain output; git add + git commit --only now scoped to the allowlist so plan edits land in the same atomic commit. Skill SKILL.md Step 10 gained a 'Script scope (T17)' note + v0.2 version history row. Smoke-tested: pathspec catches modifications in both SESSION_CONTEXT.md and a plan file, ignores unrelated paths.

title: Layer 2 — Ambient Intelligence (5 hook wirings)
description: PLAN.md Layer 2 — GATED, D19 closed with SKIP. Re-engagement requires fresh D34 approval per original gating mechanism. Contents: 2.1 user-prompt-handler.js Phase A extraction (stub runAnalyze, pending-alerts.json) — flips §4.5 frustration rule to enforced; 2.2 post-read-handler.js (PostToolUse Read matcher); 2.3 loop-detector.js (new PostToolUseFailure event section — NOT PostToolUse per research correction) — flips §4.9 loop rule to enforced; 2.4 governance-logger.js (PostToolUse Write/Edit matcher on CLAUDE.md/settings.json); 2.5 pre-commit-agent-compliance.js + track-agent-invocation.js prereq (D38) — flips §4.15 agent-result rule to enforced. Effort: ~3-4h total including pre-analysis. Trigger conditions for re-engagement: any one of (a) honor-only guardrail #5/#9/#15 violation recurs, (b) multi-user context emerges (team), (c) Step 6 sync-mechanism plan depends on governance-logger capture. Each sub-item is its own port per D18; port-agent dispatchable per D17.

title: Layer 3 — Navigation Documents (4 nav docs)
description: PLAN.md Layer 3 — GATED, D19 closed with SKIP. Re-engagement requires fresh D34 approval. Contents: 3.1 AI_WORKFLOW.md (D28, ~180-220 lines; brainstorm→research→plan→execute chain + Navigation Map + PR-review section per D22 + canonical-memory cp onboarding per D27); 3.2 .claude/COMMAND_REFERENCE.md (D32; 9 skills + 4 wired hooks + deny rules + [PLANNED] rows); 3.3 .claude/skills/SKILL_INDEX.md (D7/D26/D32; 6-col schema: name, description, portability_status, source_path, source_version, last_synced + 4 frontmatter fields on 9 skills); 3.4 .claude/HOOKS.md (D32; current 4 wired hooks + [PLANNED] rows for commit-tracker, pre-compaction-save, compact-restore, user-prompt-handler, loop-detector, governance-logger, post-read-handler, pre-commit-agent-compliance, track-agent-invocation). Effort: ~3-4h total. IMPORTANT — DO NOT RUN 3.3 BEFORE STEP 6: the 6-col schema (portability_status, source_version, last_synced) is a subset of what the R-frpg research file-registry.jsonl defines, so running 3.3 before sync-mechanism brainstorm means picking the schema twice. Trigger conditions: (a) 3.1/3.2/3.4 — when onboarding a second operator or context-switching away for 30+ days; (b) 3.3 — AFTER Step 6 sync-mechanism brainstorm produces the canonical registry schema.

title: Layer 4.1 — systematic-debugging skill port
description: PLAN.md Layer 4 item 4.1 — GATED, D19 closed with SKIP. Re-engagement requires fresh D34 approval. Source: SoNash 41526:.claude/skills/systematic-debugging/ (multi-file: SKILL.md 12KB + root-cause-tracing.md + defense-in-depth.md + condition-based-waiting.md + condition-based-waiting-example.ts + find-polluter.sh). Research value rating: 9/10 portable. Workflow: 5 phases enforcing Iron Law (NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST) — reproduce → hypothesize → isolate → fix at root cause → verify. Triggers: bug, test failure, unexpected behavior, performance issue, build failure. In JASON-OS context: when a hook fires wrong (e.g., T16 settings-guardian), skill misbehaves, or port yields unexpected behavior. Effort: ~30 min port (sanitize-then-copy at most; SoNash coupling unknown until MI-1 pre-analysis runs). Port trigger: first gnarly bug or test-failure in JASON-OS where I catch myself guessing at fixes instead of tracing root cause. Until then, keep as opportunistic.

title: Layer 4.2 — validate-claude-folder skill port
description: PLAN.md Layer 4 item 4.2 — GATED, D19 closed with SKIP. Re-engagement requires fresh D34 approval. Source: SoNash 41526:.claude/skills/validate-claude-folder/SKILL.md (3KB single file). Research value rating: 8/10 portable. 6 consistency checks: (1) .mcp.json valid JSON + server list, (2) every settings.json hook exists as a file, (3) skill ↔ command alignment, (4) COMMAND_REFERENCE.md + HOOKS.md freshness, (5) secrets config sanity, (6) agent frontmatter valid. Output: ✅/❌ summary table + specific fix commands. CAVEAT: checks (3) and (4) depend on COMMAND_REFERENCE.md + HOOKS.md which are Layer 3.2/3.4 deliverables; if Layer 3 never engages, those 2 checks stay disabled or fail noisily. Effort: ~30 min port + adaptation. Port trigger: first .claude/ drift issue (hook referenced in settings.json but file missing, skill/command name mismatch, stale doc dates) OR when Layer 3.2/3.4 engages (whichever first). Until then, keep as opportunistic.

title: Adjust /deep-research to do per-category line-count allocation for internal codebase scans
description: Standard formula (D + 3 + floor(D/5)) is tuned for external/web research where sub-question scope is roughly uniform. For internal codebase scans (e.g., sync-mechanism Piece 1 census, map-codebase variants) the formula undersizes by 2-3x because real scope varies wildly per category (13 JASON-OS skills vs 81 SoNash skills; 4 JASON-OS scripts vs 98 SoNash scripts). Revise skill Phase 0.6 to: (1) detect internal-only topic (all sub-questions are codebase-facing, not web/academic), (2) run a pre-allocation count pass on each sub-question's target directory (ls top-level + find total files), (3) size agents to <=20 meaningful units each with split when above. Observed 2026-04-18 during piece-1-discovery-scan: initial 14-agent allocation was 2-3x undersized; user caught it with 'are we sure 4 is enough?' and forced a count pass that revealed 37 agents needed. Without the user's instinct, would have shipped a partial census.

title: Fix verifier/challenger agents losing findings (no Write tool + truncation)
description: During /deep-research piece-1a-discovery-scan, the deep-research-verifier, contrarian-challenger, and otb-challenger agents were spawned but their findings were partially lost: (1) these agents lack the Write tool per their definitions, so they can't persist findings directly; (2) their response text was truncated, losing most of the per-claim verdicts from V1/V2/V3 and losing contrarian output entirely; (3) only OTB returned full output inline which the orchestrator manually persisted. Fix options: (a) grant Write tool to these agents, (b) modify /deep-research skill Phase 2.5/3 to wrap each spawn with a post-completion Write step that persists the full agent return text. Option (b) is safer (doesn't change agent permissions) and handles the Windows 0-byte fallback pattern uniformly. Observed 2026-04-18.
progress: Fixed Session 6 (2026-04-18): Write tool added to all 3 agents (deep-research-verifier, contrarian-challenger, otb-challenger) in both JASON-OS and SoNash. SKILL.md Phase 2.5 grew Persistence safety net (orchestrator captures full return + fallback-writes if agent failed/truncated); cross-refs in Phase 3 + 3.96. Mirror commit applied to sonash-v0 same session. New Claude Code session required for agent tool permissions to load.

title: T23 follow-up — grant Write to dispute-resolver + extend persistence safety net to Phase 3.5
description: T23 (Session 6) granted Write tool to deep-research-verifier, contrarian-challenger, and otb-challenger, and added the Phase 2.5 persistence safety net to /deep-research SKILL.md with cross-refs in Phase 3 and 3.96. dispute-resolver (Phase 3.5) was missed — its current grant is only Read, Grep, Glob (no Write, no Bash). During piece-1b-discovery-scan-sonash Phase 3.5 the dispute-resolver returned full narrative inline but could not persist; orchestrator fell back to manual Write. Fix: (a) update .claude/agents/dispute-resolver.md tool grant to Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob (matches deep-research-final-synthesizer shape); (b) extend /deep-research SKILL.md Phase 3.5 to apply the Phase 2.5 persistence safety net; (c) mirror commit to SoNash per Session 6 T23 precedent so repo definitions stay structurally identical. In-session caveat: agent tool grants only load at session start, so the fix takes effect next /deep-research run.
progress: Fixed Session 7 (2026-04-18): dispute-resolver grant Read,Grep,Glob -> Read,Write,Bash,Grep,Glob; SKILL.md Phase 3.5 grew persistence safety net note + version 1.10 history entry. Expanded scope with 4 enhancement grants: contrarian-challenger +Bash+WebFetch, otb-challenger +Bash+WebFetch, deep-research-gap-pursuer +context7 MCP tools, deep-research-synthesizer +Grep+Glob. Mirror commit applied to sonash-v0 same session. New Claude Code session required for agent tool permissions to load.

title: Port T17 session-end-commit.js allowlist + --no-push fix to SoNash
description: JASON-OS Session 10 (2026-04-20) shipped T17: scripts/session-end-commit.js now commits the full session-end allowlist (SESSION_CONTEXT.md + .planning/<topic>/PLAN.md + .planning/<topic>/PORT_ANALYSIS.md) in a single atomic commit instead of only SESSION_CONTEXT.md, and honors --no-push per the skill contract. SoNash is the upstream source (v2.2 lineage) and carries the same bug — /session-end skill Step 3 edits to PLAN.md/PORT_ANALYSIS.md become straggler commits there too. Port scope: (a) copy SESSION_END_PATHSPECS constant + getModifiedSessionEndFiles() function, (b) replace hasSessionContextChanges() usage in main() with stagableFiles flow, (c) scope git add + git commit --only to the allowlist, (d) add --no-push argv parsing, (e) mirror SKILL.md Step 10 'Script scope' note + version history row. Caveats: SoNash may have additional allowlist entries (PORT_ANALYSIS equivalents, ROADMAP.md, etc.) — MI-1 pre-analysis required. SoNash script may also have extra features (override logging, health scoring) — preserve them; the change is purely scope-widening.
